evidence_excerpt: With maintenance IVIg (eventually every 4 weeks) she avoided relapses and could function normally, but focal/asymmetrical disease and cramps/fasciculations persisted over 10 years.

quality_of_life.evidence_excerpt: Over 10 years, cramps and fasciculations persisted but were limited to clinically affected muscles.

timeline_events.evidence_excerpt: Two days after treatment began, there was improvement in strength that peaked by 2 weeks.
Improvement was sustained for 4 months, when the symptoms recurred.
She was re-treated with 2 g/kg of IVIg over 2 days.
After re-treatment, she again showed marked improvement in strength by 2 weeks.
She continued IVIg every few months, then every 8 weeks, and finally every 4 weeks to avoid relapses.
Maintenance IVIg sustained benefit, enabling her to function normally.

notes_on_uncertainty: The index time was proxied by the initial definitive evaluation/diagnosis in 1990. Exact dates for subsequent treatments are not provided; timing was categorized based on narrative intervals (weeks, months, and the 10-year follow-up). No death or major complication was reported; side effects were limited to transient headaches.
